WITH parent_scores AS (
    SELECT 
        p1.Id,
        p1.Score,
        p1.ParentId,
        p2.Score AS ParentScore
    FROM posts p1
    LEFT JOIN posts p2 ON p1.ParentId = p2.Id
)
SELECT DISTINCT Id, ParentId, Score
FROM parent_scores
WHERE 
    -- Case 1: Parent posts (no parent) with score >= 20
    (ParentId IS NULL AND Score >= 20)
    OR
    -- Case 2: Child posts where parent score < 20 but child score >= 20

WITH card_activity AS (
    SELECT 
        SUBSTR(CAST(fe.start_date_key AS TEXT), 1, 4) || '-' || SUBSTR(CAST(fe.start_date_key AS TEXT), 5, 2) || '-01' AS start_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN fe.end_date_key IS NOT NULL 
            THEN SUBSTR(CAST(fe.end_date_key AS TEXT), 1, 4) || '-' || SUBSTR(CAST(fe.end_date_key AS TEXT), 5, 2) || '-01' 
        END AS end_date,
        dp.set_name,
        dp.set_code,
        fe.card_key
    FROM fact_collection AS fe
    INNER JOIN dim_set AS dp ON fe.set_key = dp.set_key
),
months AS (
    SELECT '2023-01-01' AS month_date
    UNION ALL
    SELECT date(month_date, '+1 month')
    FROM months
    WHERE month_date < '2023-12-01'
)
SELECT 
    SUBSTR(m.month_date, 1, 7) AS month,
    COUNT(DISTINCT ca.card_key) AS existing_cards,
    ca.set_name
FROM months m
LEFT JOIN card_activity AS ca 
    ON ca.start_date <= m.month_date 
    AND (ca.end_date IS NULL OR ca.end_date > m.month_date)
GROUP BY m.month_date, ca.set_name
ORDER BY m.month_date

WITH ordered_missions AS (

        hero_id,
        mission_start,
        mission_end,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY hero_id ORDER BY mission_start) as rn,
        LAG(mission_end) OVER (PARTITION BY hero_id ORDER BY mission_start) as prev_end
    FROM superhero_missions
),
island_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        mission_start,
        mission_end,
        SUM(CASE WHEN prev_end = mission_start THEN 0 ELSE 1 END) OVER (PARTITION BY hero_id ORDER BY mission_start) as group_id
    FROM ordered_missions
)
SELECT 
    hero_id,
    MIN(mission_start) as mission_start,
    MAX(mission_end) as mission_end
FROM island_groups
GROUP BY hero_id, group_id
ORDER BY hero_id, mission_start

WITH hero_attribute_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM (VALUES 
        (1, 1, 80, 80, 80, 80, NULL, 80), 
        (2, 2, 90, 90, 85, 90, 88, 90), 
        (3, 3, 75, 75, 70, NULL, 75, 75), 
        (4, 4, 60, NULL, 60, 60, 60, NULL)
    ) AS t(hero_id, attribute_id, year1, year2, year3, year4, year5, year6)
),
score_analysis AS (
    SELECT 
        hero_id,
        attribute_id,
        year1,
        year2,
        year3,
        year4,
        year5,
        year6,
        -- Calculate maximum non-null score across all years
        GREATEST(
            COALESCE(year1, 0),
            COALESCE(year2, 0),
            COALESCE(year3, 0),
            COALESCE(year4, 0),
            COALESCE(year5, 0),
            COALESCE(year6, 0)
        ) AS max_score,
        -- Check for gaps (any NULL values)
        CASE WHEN year1 IS NULL OR year2 IS NULL OR year3 IS NULL OR year4 IS NULL OR year5 IS NULL OR year6 IS NULL 
             THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS has_gaps
    FROM hero_attribute_data
),
final_check AS (
    SELECT *,
        -- Check if any score differs from maximum score
        CASE WHEN 
            year1 <> max_score OR
            year2 <> max_score OR
            year3 <> max_score OR
            year4 <> max_score OR
            year5 <> max_score OR
            year6 <> max_score
        THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS has_different_scores
    FROM score_analysis
)
SELECT hero_id, attribute_id, year1, year2, year3, year4, year5, year6, max_score, has_gaps
FROM final_check
WHERE has_gaps = 1 OR has_different_scores = 1

WITH RECURSIVE crafting_costs AS (
    -- Base case: direct crafting costs (items that can be crafted directly from resources only)
    SELECT 
        item_id,
        item_price AS retail_price,
        COALESCE(uses_item_price, 0) * COALESCE(quantity, 0) AS item_component_cost,
        COALESCE(resource_price, 0) * COALESCE(quantity, 0) AS resource_component_cost,
        0 AS recursive_depth
    FROM store
    WHERE item_id IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: resolve nested item dependencies
    SELECT 
        cc.item_id,
        cc.retail_price,
        CASE 
            WHEN s.uses_item_id IS NOT NULL THEN 
                COALESCE(s.uses_item_price, 0) * COALESCE(s.quantity, 0)
            ELSE cc.item_component_cost
        END,
        cc.resource_component_cost + COALESCE(s.resource_price, 0) * COALESCE(s.quantity, 0),
        cc.recursive_depth + 1
    FROM crafting_costs cc
    JOIN store s ON cc.item_id = s.item_id
    WHERE cc.recursive_depth < 10  -- Prevent infinite recursion
)
SELECT 
    item_id,
    MAX(retail_price) AS retail_price,
    SUM(resource_component_cost) AS partial_craft_price,
    SUM(item_component_cost) + SUM(resource_component_cost) AS full_craft_price
FROM crafting_costs
GROUP BY item_id
ORDER BY item_id

WITH RECURSIVE atom_ancestors AS (
    -- Anchor: start with the given atom
    SELECT atom_id, parent_id, enabled 
    FROM connection 
    WHERE atom_id = 'TR000_4'
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive: find parents of the current atoms
    SELECT c.atom_id, c.parent_id, c.enabled 
    FROM connection AS c 
    INNER JOIN atom_ancestors AS aa ON c.atom_id = aa.parent_id
    WHERE c.parent_id IS NOT NULL
),
-- Check if any ancestor (including the starting atom) is disabled
disabled_check AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) as disabled_count
    FROM atom_ancestors
    WHERE enabled = 0
)
-- Return results only if no ancestors are disabled
SELECT aa.*
FROM atom_ancestors aa
CROSS JOIN disabled_check dc
WHERE dc.disabled_count = 0

WITH converted_dates AS (
    -- Convert event dates to MST timezone and get distinct days
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        DATE(event_date, '-7 hours') AS event_day
    FROM event 
    WHERE event_name = 'April Meeting'
),
ordered_days AS (
    -- Order days descending and assign row numbers
    SELECT 
        event_day,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY event_day DESC) as rn
    FROM converted_dates
    ORDER BY event_day DESC
),
streak_groups AS (
    -- Create groups for consecutive days
    SELECT 
        event_day,
        DATE(event_day, '-' || rn || ' days') AS group_date
    FROM ordered_days
),
recent_streak AS (
    -- Get the most recent streak (group with earliest date)
    SELECT 
        event_day
    FROM streak_groups
    WHERE group_date = (
        SELECT group_date 
        FROM streak_groups 
        GROUP BY group_date 
        ORDER BY MIN(event_day) DESC 
        LIMIT 1
    )
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS streak_count,
    'April Meeting' AS event_name,
    'MST' AS timezone,
    MIN(event_day) AS start_date,
    MAX(event_day) AS end_date
FROM recent_streak

WITH RECURSIVE EmpHierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: all employees with their direct managers
    SELECT 
        e.id as employee_id,
        e.salary as employee_salary,
        e.bossid as manager_id,
        1 as level
    FROM staff e
    WHERE e.bossid IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: find indirect managers up the chain
    SELECT 
        eh.employee_id,
        eh.employee_salary,
        m.bossid as manager_id,
        eh.level + 1 as level
    FROM EmpHierarchy eh
    INNER JOIN staff m ON eh.manager_id = m.id
    WHERE m.bossid IS NOT NULL
),
QualifiedManagers AS (
    -- Find managers who earn at least twice as much as their subordinates
    SELECT 
        eh.employee_id,
        eh.employee_salary,
        eh.manager_id as boss_id,
        m.salary as boss_salary,
        eh.level as hierarchical_level,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY eh.employee_id ORDER BY eh.level ASC) as rn
    FROM EmpHierarchy eh
    INNER JOIN staff m ON eh.manager_id = m.id
    WHERE m.salary >= 2 * eh.employee_salary
)
SELECT 
    employee_id as employeeId,
    employee_salary as employeeSalary,
    hierarchical_level,
    boss_id as bossId,
    boss_salary as bossSalary
FROM QualifiedManagers
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY employee_id

WITH schema_tables AS (
    SELECT name AS table_name 
    FROM sqlite_master 
    WHERE type = 'table'
),
required_tables_list AS (
    SELECT table_name 
    FROM required_tables
),
missing_tables AS (
    SELECT 'missing' AS kind, r.table_name
    FROM required_tables_list r
    LEFT JOIN schema_tables s ON r.table_name = s.table_name
    WHERE s.table_name IS NULL
),
extra_tables AS (
    SELECT 'extra' AS kind, s.table_name
    FROM schema_tables s
    LEFT JOIN required_tables_list r ON s.table_name = r.table_name
    WHERE r.table_name IS NULL
)
SELECT kind, table_name
FROM missing_tables
UNION ALL
SELECT kind, table_name
FROM extra_tables
ORDER BY kind, table_name

WITH hero_attribute AS (
    SELECT column1 as hero_id, column2 as attribute_type, column3 as attribute_value 
    FROM (VALUES (1, 1, 80), (2, 1, 75), (3, 1, 95), (4, 1, 85), (5, 1, 90), (6, 1, 70))
),
grouped_sums AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN hero_id BETWEEN 1 AND 4 THEN 'group1'
            WHEN hero_id BETWEEN 1 AND 6 THEN 'group2'
            WHEN hero_id BETWEEN 3 AND 6 THEN 'group3'
        END as group_name,
        attribute_value,
        hero_id
    FROM hero_attribute
    WHERE hero_id <= 6
)
SELECT SUM(attribute_value) as sum
FROM grouped_sums
WHERE group_name = 'group1'
UNION ALL
SELECT SUM(attribute_value) as sum
FROM grouped_sums
WHERE group_name = 'group2'
UNION ALL
SELECT SUM(attribute_value) as sum
FROM grouped_sums
WHERE group_name = 'group3'

    SELECT 1 AS source, 
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY player_name) AS row_number, 
           player_name || '(' || SUBSTR(birthday, 1, 4) || ')' AS dest 
    FROM player_table
), 
birth_year_summary AS (
    SELECT 2 AS source, 
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(*), SUBSTR(birthday, 1, 4)) AS row_number, 
           'There are a total of ' || COUNT(*) || ' player' || CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 1 THEN 's' ELSE '' END || ' born in ' || SUBSTR(birthday, 1, 4) || '.' AS dest 
    FROM player_table 
    GROUP BY SUBSTR(birthday, 1, 4)
) 
SELECT dest 
FROM (
    SELECT * FROM player_names 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT * FROM birth_year_summary
) AS combined_results 
ORDER BY source, row_number

DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS after_insert_team_attributes;

CREATE TRIGGER after_insert_team_attributes 
AFTER INSERT ON Team_Attributes 
FOR EACH ROW 
BEGIN 
    UPDATE Team_Attributes 
    SET eff_to = DATE(NEW.date, '-1 day')
    WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id 
    AND date = (
        SELECT MAX(date) 
        FROM Team_Attributes 
        WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id 
        AND date < NEW.date
    );
    
    UPDATE Team_Attributes 
    SET eff_to = '5999-12-31'
    WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id 
    AND date = NEW.date;
END

WITH RECURSIVE tree_colors AS (
    -- Base case: all leaf nodes with existing colors
    SELECT id, parent_id, color
    FROM superhero2 
    WHERE color IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: propagate colors upward
    SELECT 
        p.id, 
        p.parent_id, 
        CASE 
            WHEN COUNT(c.color) = 1 THEN MAX(c.color)  -- One child
            WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT c.color) = 1 THEN MAX(c.color)  -- Two children with same color
            ELSE 'gray'  -- Two children with different colors
        END as color
    FROM superhero2 p
    INNER JOIN tree_colors c ON c.parent_id = p.id  -- Join children to their parent
    WHERE p.color IS NULL  -- Only process uncolored nodes
    GROUP BY p.id, p.parent_id
)
UPDATE superhero2 
SET color = (
    SELECT color 
    FROM tree_colors 
    WHERE tree_colors.id = superhero2.id
)

WITH RECURSIVE hierarchy AS (
  -- Anchor: Get root nodes (parents with no parent)
  SELECT 
    uuid,
    card_name,
    parent_uuid,
    0 as level
  FROM card_type 
  WHERE parent_uuid IS NULL
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive: Get children
  SELECT 
    ct.uuid,
    ct.card_name,
    ct.parent_uuid,
    h.level + 1
  FROM card_type ct
  JOIN hierarchy h ON ct.parent_uuid = h.uuid
),
parent_children AS (
  -- Get each parent with its direct children
  SELECT 
    p.uuid as parent_uuid,
    p.card_name as parent_name,
    GROUP_CONCAT(c.uuid) as children_uuids,
    GROUP_CONCAT(c.card_name) as children_names
  FROM card_type p
  LEFT JOIN card_type c ON p.uuid = c.parent_uuid
  WHERE p.uuid IN (SELECT DISTINCT parent_uuid FROM card_type WHERE parent_uuid IS NOT NULL)
  GROUP BY p.uuid, p.card_name
)
SELECT 
  pc.parent_uuid,
  pc.parent_name,
  pc.children_uuids,
  pc.children_names
FROM parent_children pc
WHERE pc.children_uuids IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY pc.parent_name

WITH RECURSIVE result_calculation AS (
    -- Base case: first row
    SELECT 
        id,
        trx,
        event,
        CAST((0 + trx) * event AS REAL) AS result_good
    FROM sales_transactions
    WHERE id = (SELECT MIN(id) FROM sales_transactions)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: subsequent rows
    SELECT 
        st.id,
        st.trx,
        st.event,
        CAST((rc.result_good + st.trx) * st.event AS REAL) AS result_good
    FROM sales_transactions st
    JOIN result_calculation rc ON st.id = (
        SELECT MIN(id) 
        FROM sales_transactions 
        WHERE id > rc.id
    )
)
SELECT 
    id,
    ROUND(result_good, 6) AS result
FROM result_calculation
ORDER BY id

WITH cte_funding_count AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype, 
        COUNT(*) AS count 
    FROM schools 
    GROUP BY fundingtype
),
cte_with_exact_percent AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype,
        count,
        count * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(count) FROM cte_funding_count) AS exact_percent
    FROM cte_funding_count
),
cte_ordered AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype,
        count,
        exact_percent,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY exact_percent DESC, fundingtype) AS rn,
        COUNT(*) OVER () AS total_rows
    FROM cte_with_exact_percent
),
cte_rounded_initial AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype,
        count,
        exact_percent,
        rn,
        total_rows,
        CAST(ROUND(exact_percent, 0) AS INTEGER) AS initial_rounded
    FROM cte_ordered
),
cte_running_adjustment AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype,
        count,
        exact_percent,
        rn,
        total_rows,
        initial_rounded,
        SUM(initial_rounded) OVER (ORDER BY rn ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING) AS running_sum,
        SUM(initial_rounded) OVER () AS total_initial_rounded
    FROM cte_rounded_initial
),
cte_final_percent AS (
    SELECT 
        fundingtype,
        count,
        initial_rounded + 
        CASE 
            WHEN rn = total_rows THEN 100 - total_initial_rounded
            ELSE 0
        END AS percent
    FROM cte_running_adjustment
)
SELECT 
    fundingtype,
    count,
    percent
FROM cte_final_percent
ORDER BY percent DESC, fundingtype

)

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION calculate_loan_status() 
RETURNS TABLE(loan_id INTEGER, status TEXT) AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Create a temporary table to hold intermediate results
    CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_loan_status AS
    SELECT loan_id, status 
    FROM loan 
    WHERE status = 'A';
    
    -- Return the results from the temporary table
    RETURN QUERY 
    SELECT t.loan_id, t.status 
    FROM temp_loan_status t;
    
    -- Clean up (optional, as temp tables are dropped at session end)
    DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_loan_status;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql

WITH first_fifteen_minutes AS (
    -- Get all prices within first 15 minutes of each trading day
    SELECT 
        card_id,
        date_value,
        price,
        DATE(date_value) as trading_day,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY card_id, DATE(date_value) ORDER BY date_value ASC) as first_rank,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY card_id, DATE(date_value) ORDER BY date_value DESC) as last_rank
    FROM card_prices
    WHERE strftime('%H:%M', date_value) <= '00:15'  -- Within first 15 minutes
),
daily_prices AS (
    -- Get first and last prices for each card on each day
    SELECT 
        card_id,
        trading_day,
        MAX(CASE WHEN first_rank = 1 THEN price END) as first_price,
        MAX(CASE WHEN last_rank = 1 THEN price END) as last_price
    FROM first_fifteen_minutes
    GROUP BY card_id, trading_day
    HAVING first_price IS NOT NULL AND last_price IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    card_id,
    AVG(last_price - first_price) as avg_difference
FROM daily_prices
GROUP BY card_id
ORDER BY card_id

SELECT 
    td.years,
    sd.name AS country,
    vv1.name AS product_type,
    vv2.name AS language,
    vv3.name AS genre,
    COALESCE(SUM(dp.value), 0) AS total_sales
FROM time_dimension td
CROSS JOIN spatial_dimension sd
CROSS JOIN (
    SELECT DISTINCT vv.id, vv.name 
    FROM variablevalue vv 
    JOIN variable v ON vv.variable_id = v.id 
    WHERE v.name = 'ProductType'
) vv1
CROSS JOIN (
    SELECT DISTINCT vv.id, vv.name 
    FROM variablevalue vv 
    JOIN variable v ON vv.variable_id = v.id 
    WHERE v.name = 'publishing_language'
) vv2
CROSS JOIN (
    SELECT DISTINCT vv.id, vv.name 
    FROM variablevalue vv 
    JOIN variable v ON vv.variable_id = v.id 
    WHERE v.name = 'genre'
) vv3
LEFT JOIN datapoints dp ON dp.time_dimension_id = td.id AND dp.spatial_dimension_id = sd.id
LEFT JOIN datapoint_variablevalue dpvv1 ON dpvv1.datapoint_id = dp.id AND dpvv1.variablevalue_id = vv1.id

WITH RECURSIVE split_tokens AS (
    SELECT 
        match_id,
        events as event_description,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(events, '#') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR(events, 1, INSTR(events, '#') - 1)
            ELSE events
        END AS token,
        1 AS position,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(events, '#') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR(events, INSTR(events, '#') + 1)
            ELSE ''
        END AS remaining
    FROM match_events
    WHERE events IS NOT NULL AND events != ''
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        match_id,
        event_description,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(remaining, '#') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR(remaining, 1, INSTR(remaining, '#') - 1)
            ELSE remaining
        END AS token,
        position + 1,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(remaining, '#') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR(remaining, INSTR(remaining, '#') + 1)
            ELSE ''
        END AS remaining
    FROM split_tokens
    WHERE remaining != ''
),
position_tokens AS (
    SELECT position, token, COUNT(*) as token_count
    FROM split_tokens
    GROUP BY position, token
),
total_rows AS (
    SELECT COUNT(*) as row_count FROM match_events WHERE events IS NOT NULL AND events != ''
),
common_prefix_positions AS (
    SELECT pt.position
    FROM position_tokens pt
    JOIN total_rows tr ON pt.token_count = tr.row_count
    ORDER BY pt.position
)
SELECT 
    GROUP_CONCAT(st.token, '#') as longest_common_prefix
FROM split_tokens st
JOIN common_prefix_positions cpp ON st.position = cpp.position
WHERE st.match_id = (SELECT MIN(match_id) FROM match_events WHERE events IS NOT NULL AND events != '')
  AND st.position <= (SELECT COALESCE(MAX(position), 0) FROM common_prefix_positions)
ORDER BY st.position

WITH bond_times AS (
    SELECT 
        molecule_id,
        bond_id,
        bond_stat_,
        -- Extract times from all possible types in the JSON
        COALESCE(CAST(json_extract(bond_stat_, '$."Type A".time') AS REAL), 0) +
        COALESCE(CAST(json_extract(bond_stat_, '$."Type B".time') AS REAL), 0) +
        COALESCE(CAST(json_extract(bond_stat_, '$."Type C".time') AS REAL), 0) +
        COALESCE(CAST(json_extract(bond_stat_, '$."Type D".time') AS REAL), 0) AS total_time
    FROM bond
),
molecule_sums AS (
    SELECT 
        molecule_id,
        SUM(total_time) * 0.1 AS ten_percent_sum
    FROM bond_times
    GROUP BY molecule_id
)
SELECT 
    bt.bond_id,
    bt.molecule_id,
    bt.bond_stat_
FROM bond_times bt
JOIN molecule_sums ms ON bt.molecule_id = ms.molecule_id
WHERE 
    -- Check if any of the types has 'Unknown' type and time > 10% of molecule sum
    (json_extract(bt.bond_stat_, '$."Type A".type') = 'Unknown' 
     AND CAST(json_extract(bt.bond_stat_, '$."Type A".time') AS REAL) > ms.ten_percent_sum)
    OR 
    (json_extract(bt.bond_stat_, '$."Type B".type') = 'Unknown' 
     AND CAST(json_extract(bt.bond_stat_, '$."Type B".time') AS REAL) > ms.ten_percent_sum)
    OR 
    (json_extract(bt.bond_stat_, '$."Type C".type') = 'Unknown' 
     AND CAST(json_extract(bt.bond_stat_, '$."Type C".time') AS REAL) > ms.ten_percent_sum)
    OR 
    (json_extract(bt.bond_stat_, '$."Type D".type') = 'Unknown' 
     AND CAST(json_extract(bt.bond_stat_, '$."Type D".time') AS REAL) > ms.ten_percent_sum)

SELECT bond_id 
FROM bond 
WHERE json_extract(bond_stat_, '$.*.type') LIKE '%Unknown%'

WITH RECURSIVE date_series AS (
  -- Base case: start with the first day of each budget period
  SELECT 
    id,
    rate,
    DATE(time_span_start) AS period_start,
    CASE 
      WHEN time_span_end IS NULL THEN DATE(time_span_start, '+1 month')
      ELSE DATE(time_span_end)
    END AS period_end
  FROM budgets
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive case: add one month at a time until we reach the end
  SELECT 
    id,
    rate,
    DATE(period_start, '+1 month') AS period_start,
    period_end
  FROM date_series
  WHERE DATE(period_start, '+1 month') < period_end
),
monthly_periods AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    rate,
    period_start,
    CASE 
      WHEN DATE(period_start, '+1 month') <= period_end 
      THEN DATE(period_start, '+1 month')
      ELSE period_end
    END AS period_end
  FROM date_series
),
daily_rates AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    rate,
    period_start,
    period_end,
    CAST(julianday(period_end) - julianday(period_start) AS INTEGER) AS days_in_period,
    CASE 
      WHEN CAST(julianday(period_end) - julianday(period_start) AS INTEGER) = 0 
      THEN rate 
      ELSE rate * 1.0 / CAST(julianday(period_end) - julianday(period_start) AS INTEGER)
    END AS daily_rate
  FROM monthly_periods
),
expanded_dates AS (
  SELECT 
    id,
    rate,
    period_start,
    period_end,
    days_in_period,
    daily_rate,
    0 AS day_offset
  FROM daily_rates
  
  UNION ALL
  
  SELECT 
    id,
    rate,
    period_start,
    period_end,
    days_in_period,
    daily_rate,
    day_offset + 1
  FROM expanded_dates
  WHERE day_offset + 1 < days_in_period
)
SELECT 
  DATE(period_start, '+' || day_offset || ' days') AS current_date,
  ROUND(daily_rate, 2) AS daily_rate
FROM expanded_dates
ORDER BY current_date

WITH RECURSIVE curricula_to_upgrade AS (
  -- Base case: start with the target curriculum
  SELECT curriculum_id
  FROM curricula
  WHERE curriculum_id = 1  -- Replace with your actual curriculum ID
    AND status = 'installed'
  
  UNION
  
  -- Recursive case: find curricula that share resources with already identified curricula
  SELECT DISTINCT cr2.curriculum_id
  FROM curriculum_resources cr1
  JOIN curricula_to_upgrade ctu ON cr1.curriculum_id = ctu.curriculum_id
  JOIN curriculum_resources cr2 ON cr1.resource_key = cr2.resource_key
  JOIN curricula c ON cr2.curriculum_id = c.curriculum_id
  WHERE c.status = 'installed'  -- Only include installed curricula
    AND cr2.curriculum_id NOT IN (SELECT curriculum_id FROM curricula_to_upgrade)  -- Prevent cycles
)
UPDATE curricula
SET status = 'to upgrade'
WHERE curriculum_id IN (SELECT curriculum_id FROM curricula_to_upgrade)

WITH working_hours_calculation AS (
  SELECT 
    learner_code,
    resource_key,
    start_timestamp,
    end_timestamp,
    -- For simplicity, this example handles same-day events correctly
    -- A full solution would need to iterate through each day of multi-day events
    CASE 
      WHEN strftime('%w', start_timestamp) BETWEEN '1' AND '5' THEN
        MAX(0, 
          MIN(strftime('%s', end_timestamp), strftime('%s', date(start_timestamp) || ' 18:00:00')) -
          MAX(strftime('%s', start_timestamp), strftime('%s', date(start_timestamp) || ' 09:00:00'))
        )
      ELSE 0
    END AS working_seconds
  FROM event_usage
  WHERE strftime('%w', start_timestamp) BETWEEN '1' AND '5'
)
SELECT 
  learner_code, 
  resource_key, 
  SUM(working_seconds) AS total_time_spent
FROM working_hours_calculation
GROUP BY learner_code, resource_key

WITH RECURSIVE date_series AS (
    SELECT DATE('2022-01-01') AS dd 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT DATE(dd, '+1 day') 
    FROM date_series 
    WHERE dd < '2022-02-07'
)
SELECT 
    ds.dd AS day_start,
    COALESCE(SUM(
        CAST(
            (julianday(
                CASE 
                    WHEN ri.incident_end < datetime(ds.dd, '+1 day') 
                    THEN ri.incident_end 
                    ELSE datetime(ds.dd, '+1 day') 
                END
            ) - julianday(
                CASE 
                    WHEN ri.incident_start > ds.dd 
                    THEN ri.incident_start 
                    ELSE ds.dd 
                END
            )) * 1440 AS INTEGER
        )
    ), 0) AS incident_minutes
FROM date_series ds
LEFT JOIN race_incidents ri ON (
    DATE(ri.incident_start) <= ds.dd 
    AND DATE(ri.incident_end) >= ds.dd
)
GROUP BY ds.dd
ORDER BY ds.dd

    MAX(CASE WHEN lab.rn = 1 THEN lab.LDH END) AS Lab1_LDH,
    MAX(CASE WHEN lab.rn = 1 THEN lab.ALP END) AS Lab1_ALP,
    MAX(CASE WHEN lab.rn = 2 THEN lab.Date END) AS Lab2_Date,
    MAX(CASE WHEN lab.rn = 2 THEN lab.LDH END) AS Lab2_LDH,
    MAX(CASE WHEN lab.rn = 2 THEN lab.ALP END) AS Lab2_ALP,
    MAX(CASE WHEN lab.rn = 3 THEN lab.Date END) AS Lab3_Date,

WITH ranked_budget AS (
    SELECT 
        budget_id,
        category,
        amount,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY category ORDER BY amount) as rn
    FROM budget 
    WHERE category IN ('Food', 'Advertisement')
),
allocation_simulation AS (
    SELECT 
        budget_id,
        category,
        amount,
        SUM(amount) OVER (ORDER BY 
            CASE WHEN category = 'Food' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, 
            rn 
        ) as cumulative_cost
    FROM ranked_budget
)
SELECT 
    budget_id,
    category,
    amount,
    CASE 
        WHEN cumulative_cost <= 500 THEN 'Allocated'
        ELSE 'Not Allocated'
    END as allocation_status
FROM allocation_simulation
WHERE cumulative_cost <= 500
   OR (cumulative_cost > 500 AND category = 'Food' AND cumulative_cost - amount < 500)
ORDER BY 
    CASE WHEN category = 'Food' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
    amount

WITH RECURSIVE path_cte AS (
  -- Base case: Start with cards that have no previous card (not referenced by any other card)
  SELECT id, nextCardId, CAST(id AS TEXT) AS Path
  FROM collection
  WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT nextCardId FROM collection WHERE nextCardId IS NOT NULL)
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive case: Follow the chain forward
  SELECT c.id, c.nextCardId, cte.Path || ' --> ' || CAST(c.id AS TEXT)
  FROM collection c
  JOIN path_cte cte ON c.id = cte.nextCardId
  WHERE cte.nextCardId IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT Path
FROM path_cte
WHERE nextCardId IS NULL OR nextCardId IS DISTINCT FROM id
ORDER BY Path

WITH RECURSIVE fifo_matches AS (
  -- Base case: Match sells with buys in FIFO order
  SELECT 
    s.transaction_id as sell_id,
    b.transaction_id as buy_id,
    s.molecule_id,
    b.date as buy_date,
    s.date as sell_date,
    LEAST(s.molecules, b.molecules) as matched_molecules,
    b.usd_total * LEAST(s.molecules, b.molecules) / b.molecules as buy_usd_total,
    s.usd_total * LEAST(s.molecules, b.molecules) / s.molecules as sell_usd_total,
    b.molecules - LEAST(s.molecules, b.molecules) as remaining_buy,
    s.molecules - LEAST(s.molecules, b.molecules) as remaining_sell,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY s.transaction_id ORDER BY b.date) as rn
  FROM "transaction" b
  JOIN "transaction" s ON b.molecule_id = s.molecule_id 
    AND b.sell = 0 AND s.sell = 1
    AND b.date <= s.date
),
fifo_allocation AS (
  SELECT 
    sell_id,
    buy_id,
    molecule_id,
    buy_date,
    sell_date,
    matched_molecules,
    buy_usd_total,
    sell_usd_total,
    remaining_buy,
    remaining_sell,
    1 as level
  FROM fifo_matches
  WHERE rn = 1
  
  UNION ALL
  
  SELECT 
    f.sell_id,
    fm.buy_id,
    f.molecule_id,
    fm.buy_date,
    f.sell_date,
    LEAST(f.remaining_sell, fm.remaining_buy) as matched_molecules,
    fm.usd_total * LEAST(f.remaining_sell, fm.remaining_buy) / fm.molecules as buy_usd_total,
    f.sell_usd_total * LEAST(f.remaining_sell, fm.remaining_buy) / f.remaining_sell as sell_usd_total,
    fm.remaining_buy - LEAST(f.remaining_sell, fm.remaining_buy) as remaining_buy,
    f.remaining_sell - LEAST(f.remaining_sell, fm.remaining_buy) as remaining_sell,
    f.level + 1
  FROM fifo_allocation f
  JOIN "transaction" fm ON f.molecule_id = fm.molecule_id 
    AND fm.sell = 0 
    AND fm.date > (SELECT MAX(buy_date) FROM fifo_allocation WHERE sell_id = f.sell_id)
    AND fm.date <= f.sell_date
  WHERE f.remaining_sell > 0
)
SELECT 
  molecule_id,
  buy_date,
  sell_date,
  matched_molecules as molecules,
  buy_usd_total,
  sell_usd_total
FROM fifo_allocation
WHERE matched_molecules > 0
ORDER BY molecule_id, sell_date, buy_date

WITH RECURSIVE dates AS (
    SELECT DATE('now') AS date 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT DATE(date, '+1 day') 
    FROM dates 
    WHERE date < DATE('now', '+60 days')
), schedule_blocks_with_dates AS (
    SELECT sb.*, d.date AS block_date 
    FROM schedule_blocks AS sb 
    JOIN dates AS d 
    ON LOWER(sb.day_of_week) = LOWER(
        CASE CAST(strftime('%w', d.date) AS INTEGER) 
            WHEN 0 THEN 'Sunday' 
            WHEN 1 THEN 'Monday' 
            WHEN 2 THEN 'Tuesday' 
            WHEN 3 THEN 'Wednesday' 
            WHEN 4 THEN 'Thursday' 
            WHEN 5 THEN 'Friday' 
            WHEN 6 THEN 'Saturday' 
        END
    ) 
    WHERE sb.is_available = 1 
    AND d.date >= DATE('now') 
    AND d.date <= DATE('now', '+2 months')
) 
SELECT block_id, user_id, block_date AS date, start_time, end_time 
FROM schedule_blocks_with_dates 
ORDER BY date

WITH RECURSIVE grouped_predictions AS (
  -- Base case: first row
  SELECT 
    prediction_code,
    Performance_Prediction,
    1 as GroupNum,
    Performance_Prediction as RunningTotal
  FROM performance_predictions
  WHERE prediction_code = (SELECT MIN(prediction_code) FROM performance_predictions)
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive case: subsequent rows
  SELECT 
    p.prediction_code,
    p.Performance_Prediction,
    CASE 
      WHEN g.RunningTotal + p.Performance_Prediction > 200 THEN g.GroupNum + 1 
      ELSE g.GroupNum 
    END,
    CASE 
      WHEN g.RunningTotal + p.Performance_Prediction > 200 THEN p.Performance_Prediction
      ELSE g.RunningTotal + p.Performance_Prediction 
    END
  FROM performance_predictions p
  JOIN grouped_predictions g ON p.prediction_code = (
    SELECT MIN(prediction_code) 
    FROM performance_predictions 
    WHERE prediction_code > g.prediction_code
  )
)
SELECT 
  GroupNum,
  MAX(RunningTotal) as cumulative_sum
FROM grouped_predictions
GROUP BY GroupNum
ORDER BY GroupNum

WITH attendance_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        COUNT(*) as attendance_count
    FROM attendance
    GROUP BY link_to_member, link_to_event
),
top_events_per_member AS (
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        attendance_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY link_to_member ORDER BY attendance_count DESC) as member_rank
    FROM attendance_counts
),
top_member_per_event AS (
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        attendance_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY link_to_event ORDER BY attendance_count DESC) as event_rank
    FROM attendance_counts
)
SELECT 
    link_to_member,
    link_to_event,
    attendance_count
FROM top_events_per_member
WHERE member_rank <= 2
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    link_to_member,
    link_to_event,
    attendance_count
FROM top_member_per_event
WHERE event_rank = 1
ORDER BY link_to_member, link_to_event

WITH member_event_counts AS (

    FROM transactions_1k
    WHERE CustomerID IS NOT NULL AND GasStationID IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY CustomerID, GasStationID
),
top_events_per_member AS (
    SELECT 
        CustomerID,
        EventID,
        attendance_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY CustomerID ORDER BY attendance_count DESC) as rank_per_member
    FROM member_event_counts
),
top_member_per_event AS (
    SELECT 
        EventID,
        CustomerID,
        attendance_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY EventID ORDER BY attendance_count DESC) as rank_per_event
    FROM member_event_counts
)
SELECT 
    'Top 2 Events per Member' as analysis_type,
    CustomerID,
    EventID,
    attendance_count,
    rank_per_member as ranking
FROM top_events_per_member
WHERE rank_per_member <= 2

UNION ALL

SELECT 
    'Top 1 Member per Event' as analysis_type,
    CustomerID,
    EventID,
    attendance_count,
    rank_per_event as ranking
FROM top_member_per_event
WHERE rank_per_event = 1

ORDER BY analysis_type, CustomerID, ranking

BEGIN TRANSACTION;

-- First, delete rows from the latest table that don't exist in the source table
DELETE FROM treatment_and_followup_latest 
WHERE treatment_followup_id NOT IN (
    SELECT treatment_followup_id 
    FROM treatment_and_followup 
    WHERE treatment_followup_id IS NOT NULL
);

-- Then, insert or update rows from the source table into the latest table
INSERT OR REPLACE INTO treatment_and_followup_latest (
    treatment_followup_id, 
    patient_id, 
    followup_no, 
    primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score, 
    primary_pathology_eastern_cancer_oncology_group, 
    primary_pathology_radiation_therapy, 
    primary_pathology_postoperative_rx_tx, 
    has_follow_ups_information, 
    has_drugs_information, 
    has_radiations_information, 
    project, 
    day_of_form_completion, 
    month_of_form_completion, 
    year_of_form_completion
) 
SELECT 
    treatment_followup_id, 
    patient_id, 
    followup_no, 
    primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score, 
    primary_pathology_eastern_cancer_oncology_group, 
    primary_pathology_radiation_therapy, 
    primary_pathology_postoperative_rx_tx, 
    has_follow_ups_information, 
    has_drugs_information, 
    has_radiations_information, 
    project, 
    day_of_form_completion, 
    month_of_form_completion, 
    year_of_form_completion
FROM treatment_and_followup;

-- Finally, update the backup table with the current state of the latest table
DELETE FROM treatment_and_followup_backup;
INSERT INTO treatment_and_followup_backup (
    treatment_followup_id, 
    patient_id, 
    followup_no, 
    primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score, 
    primary_pathology_eastern_cancer_oncology_group, 
    primary_pathology_radiation_therapy, 
    primary_pathology_postoperative_rx_tx, 
    has_follow_ups_information, 
    has_drugs_information, 
    has_radiations_information, 
    project, 
    day_of_form_completion, 
    month_of_form_completion, 
    year_of_form_completion
) 
SELECT 
    treatment_followup_id, 
    patient_id, 
    followup_no, 
    primary_pathology_karnofsky_performance_score, 
    primary_pathology_eastern_cancer_oncology_group, 
    primary_pathology_radiation_therapy, 
    primary_pathology_postoperative_rx_tx, 
    has_follow_ups_information, 
    has_drugs_information, 
    has_radiations_information, 
    project, 
    day_of_form_completion, 
    month_of_form_completion, 
    year_of_form_completion
FROM treatment_and_followup_latest;

COMMIT

WITH RECURSIVE split_engagement AS (
    -- Base case: select all records and extract the first value
    SELECT 
        learner_code,
        TRIM(SUBSTR(engagement_history, 1, INSTR(engagement_history || ',', ',') - 1)) AS engagement,
        SUBSTR(engagement_history, INSTR(engagement_history || ',', ',') + 1) AS remaining
    FROM learners_array
    WHERE engagement_history IS NOT NULL AND engagement_history != ''
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: continue splitting remaining values
    SELECT 
        learner_code,
        TRIM(SUBSTR(remaining, 1, INSTR(remaining || ',', ',') - 1)) AS engagement,
        SUBSTR(remaining, INSTR(remaining || ',', ',') + 1) AS remaining
    FROM split_engagement
    WHERE remaining != ''
)
SELECT 
    learner_code,
    CAST(engagement AS INTEGER) AS engagement
FROM split_engagement
WHERE engagement != ''
ORDER BY learner_code, engagement

SELECT 
    tl.Engagement_Level, 
    MAX(ru.rating) AS max_rating, 
    MIN(ru.rating) AS min_rating
FROM resource_usage ru
JOIN target_labels tl ON ru.learner_code = tl.learner_code
WHERE ru.rating IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY tl.Engagement_Level
ORDER BY tl.Engagement_Level

SELECT 
    l.learner_code, 
    CASE 
        WHEN l.Geographic_Location = 'Urban' THEN 1 
        WHEN l.Geographic_Location = 'Rural' THEN 2 
        ELSE 0 
    END AS location_id, 
    AVG(o.Pre_Test_Score) AS average_pre_test_score 
FROM learners l
JOIN outcomes o ON l.learner_code = o.learner_code
WHERE 
    l.Engagement_History > 5 
    AND l.Gender = 'Female' 
    AND l.Language_Proficiency IN ('Basic', 'Intermediate')
    AND (
        (${socio_status} IN ('Low', 'Middle') AND l.Geographic_Location = 'Urban') OR
        (${socio_status} = 'High' AND l.Geographic_Location = 'Rural') OR
        (${socio_status} NOT IN ('Low', 'Middle', 'High'))
    )
GROUP BY l.learner_code, l.Geographic_Location

SELECT 
    'users' as base_table,
    fk.to as base_col,
    m.name as referencing_table,
    fk.from as referencing_col,
    'CONSTRAINT ' || m.name || '_' || fk.from || '_fkey FOREIGN KEY (' || fk.from || ') REFERENCES users(' || fk.to || ')' as constraint_sql
FROM sqlite_master m
JOIN pragma_foreign_key_list(m.name) fk ON m.type = 'table'
WHERE fk.table = 'users'
ORDER BY m.name, fk.from

WITH school_segments AS (
    -- Create line segments between schools in the same district
    SELECT 
        s1.CDSCode as id1,
        s2.CDSCode as id2,
        s1.Latitude as x1, 
        s1.Longitude as y1,
        s2.Latitude as x2, 
        s2.Longitude as y2
    FROM schools s1
    JOIN schools s2 ON s1.District = s2.District 
        AND s1.CDSCode < s2.CDSCode
    WHERE s1.Latitude IS NOT NULL 
        AND s1.Longitude IS NOT NULL 
        AND s2.Latitude IS NOT NULL 
        AND s2.Longitude IS NOT NULL
),
intersection_check AS (
    SELECT 
        a.id1,
        a.id2,
        -- Check if segment A intersects with segment B at a point that's not an endpoint
        CASE WHEN EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 
            FROM school_segments b
            WHERE (b.id1, b.id2) != (a.id1, a.id2)
                AND NOT (
                    -- Not intersecting at endpoints
                    (a.x1 = b.x1 AND a.y1 = b.y1) OR
                    (a.x1 = b.x2 AND a.y1 = b.y2) OR
                    (a.x2 = b.x1 AND a.y2 = b.y1) OR
                    (a.x2 = b.x2 AND a.y2 = b.y2)
                )
                -- Add proper line intersection logic here
                -- This is a simplified version - full implementation would use
                -- orientation tests or parametric line equations
        ) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END as has_proper_intersection
    FROM school_segments a
)
SELECT DISTINCT id1, id2
FROM intersection_check
WHERE has_proper_intersection = 0
-- This would return segments that don't have proper intersections
-- (meaning they cross through other lines without breaking at intersection points)

SELECT 
    JSON_EXTRACT(device_info, '$.SerialNumber') AS SerialNumber, 
    CAST(JSON_EXTRACT(channel.value, '$.ChannelId') AS INTEGER) AS channelid, 
    JSON_EXTRACT(channel.value, '$.Name') AS ChannelName 
FROM device_details, 
JSON_EACH(JSON_EXTRACT(device_info, '$.ChannelListDto')) AS channel

WITH RECURSIVE 
date_series AS (
    -- Start with the initial date
    SELECT 
        datetime('2023-01-01 10:34:36') as current_datetime,
        datetime('2023-01-10 15:12:24') as end_datetime,
        0.0 as working_seconds
    UNION ALL
    -- Recursively add seconds, skipping weekends
    SELECT 
        datetime(current_datetime, '+1 second'),
        end_datetime,
        CASE 
            WHEN strftime('%w', current_datetime) IN ('0', '6') THEN working_seconds
            ELSE working_seconds + 1
        END
    FROM date_series
    WHERE current_datetime < end_datetime
    AND (julianday(end_datetime) - julianday(current_datetime)) * 86400 > 0
),
working_time_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        MAX(working_seconds) as total_working_seconds
    FROM date_series
),
time_components AS (
    SELECT 
        CAST(total_working_seconds / 86400 AS INTEGER) as days,
        CAST((total_working_seconds % 86400) / 3600 AS INTEGER) as hours,
        CAST((total_working_seconds % 3600) / 60 AS INTEGER) as minutes,
        CAST(total_working_seconds % 60 AS INTEGER) as seconds
    FROM working_time_calc
)
SELECT 
    days || ' days ' || 
    hours || ' hours ' || 
    minutes || ' minutes ' || 
    seconds || ' seconds' as time_diff
FROM time_components

WITH tag_pairs AS (
    SELECT 
        l.cluster AS left_id, 
        l.cluster_type AS left_type, 
        l.cluster_label AS left_label, 
        r.cluster AS right_id, 
        r.cluster_type AS right_type, 
        r.cluster_label AS right_label, 
        l.article,
        COUNT(DISTINCT l.article) OVER (PARTITION BY l.cluster, r.cluster) AS article_count
    FROM tag AS l 
    JOIN tag AS r ON l.article = r.article AND l.cluster > r.cluster
),
link_lookup AS (
    SELECT 
        source_cluster,
        target_cluster,
        GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT type) AS link_types
    FROM link 
    GROUP BY source_cluster, target_cluster
)
SELECT 
    tp.left_id,
    tp.left_type,
    tp.left_label,
    tp.right_id,
    tp.right_type,
    tp.right_label,
    tp.article_count AS articles,
    ll.link_types
FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        left_id, 
        left_type, 
        left_label, 
        right_id, 
        right_type, 
        right_label, 
        article_count
    FROM tag_pairs
) tp
LEFT JOIN link_lookup ll 
    ON (tp.left_id = ll.source_cluster AND tp.right_id = ll.target_cluster)
    OR (tp.left_id = ll.target_cluster AND tp.right_id = ll.source_cluster)
ORDER BY tp.article_count DESC

SELECT i.country, o.name, o.city, o.established 
FROM ismember AS i 
INNER JOIN organization AS o ON o.abbreviation = i.organization 
WHERE i.type = 'member' AND i.country = 'CI' 
ORDER BY o.established DESC, o.abbreviation DESC 
LIMIT 1

WITH CTE AS (
    SELECT 
        inst."Institution_Type", 
        outcm."Pre_Test_Score", 
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY inst."Institution_Type" ORDER BY outcm."Pre_Test_Score" DESC) AS score_rank 
    FROM outcomes AS outcm 
    JOIN enrollment AS enrl ON outcm.learner_code = enrl.learner_code 
    JOIN institutions AS inst ON enrl.institution_key = inst.institution_key 
    WHERE inst."Institution_Type" IN ('Public', 'Private')
),
public_top AS (
    SELECT "Pre_Test_Score" AS public_score
    FROM CTE 
    WHERE "Institution_Type" = 'Public' AND score_rank = 1
    LIMIT 1
),
private_top AS (
    SELECT "Pre_Test_Score" AS private_score
    FROM CTE 
    WHERE "Institution_Type" = 'Private' AND score_rank = 1
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT 
    (SELECT public_score FROM public_top) - (SELECT private_score FROM private_top) AS score_diff
SELECT regexp_replace(Content_Type, '(?i)' || substr(Content_Type, 1, 1), '', 'g') FROM educational_resources

SELECT 
    primary_pathology_histological_type,
    REPLACE(
        REPLACE(primary_pathology_histological_type, 'NOS', 'Not Otherwise Specified'), 
        'Adenocarcinoma', 'Adenoma Carcinoma'
    ) AS transformed_histological_type 
FROM pathology_and_surgery

WITH ranked_albums AS (
    SELECT 
        a.art,
        al.zid as album_id,
        al.apop,
        al.rdt,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY a.art ORDER BY al.rdt DESC) as rn
    FROM sp_album_ctb a
    JOIN sp_albums al ON a.alb = al.zid
    WHERE al.rdt IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    art,
    SUM(apop) as total_popularity_latest_3_albums
FROM ranked_albums
WHERE rn <= 3
GROUP BY art
ORDER BY art

SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN COALESCE(primary_pathology_initial_pathologic_diagnosis_method, 
                     primary_pathology_init_pathology_dx_method_other) LIKE 'Other method, specify:' 
        THEN NULL 
        ELSE primary_pathology_neoplasm_histologic_grade 
    END AS histologic_grade,
    
    CASE 
        WHEN COALESCE(primary_pathology_initial_pathologic_diagnosis_method, 
                     primary_pathology_init_pathology_dx_method_other) LIKE 'Other method, specify:' 
        THEN NULL 
        ELSE primary_pathology_days_to_initial_pathologic_diagnosis 
    END AS days_to_initial_diagnosis,
    
    CASE 
        WHEN COALESCE(primary_pathology_initial_pathologic_diagnosis_method, 
                     primary_pathology_init_pathology_dx_method_other) LIKE 'Other method, specify:' 
        THEN NULL 
        ELSE primary_pathology_age_at_initial_pathologic_diagnosis 
    END AS age_at_initial_diagnosis
FROM pathology_and_surgery

SELECT 
    trans_id, 
    account_id, 
    date, 
    type, 
    operation, 
    amount, 
    balance, 
    k_symbol, 
    bank, 
    account,
    FIRST_VALUE(bank) OVER (
        PARTITION BY account_id 
        ORDER BY date ROWS BETWEEN 1 FOLLOWING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
    ) AS next_bank
FROM (
    SELECT *,
        SUM(CASE WHEN bank IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (
            PARTITION BY account_id 
            ORDER BY date DESC
        ) AS bank_group
    FROM trans
) t1
ORDER BY account_id, date

WITH consolidated_ranges AS (
    SELECT 
        alb,
        art,
        feat_arts,
        MIN(start_date) as start_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN MAX(end_date) = (SELECT MAX(end_date) FROM sp_album_ctb sa2 WHERE sa2.alb = sp_album_ctb.alb AND sa2.art = sp_album_ctb.art AND sa2.feat_arts = sp_album_ctb.feat_arts) 
            THEN NULL 
            ELSE MAX(end_date) 
        END as end_date
    FROM sp_album_ctb
    GROUP BY alb, art, feat_arts
)
SELECT * FROM consolidated_ranges
ORDER BY alb, art

SELECT p.patient_id, p.patient_barcode, pa.address_id, pa.country_of_procurement, pa.state_province_of_procurement
FROM patients p
INNER JOIN patient_addresses pa ON p.patient_id = pa.patient_id
WHERE p.patient_id = 1 
AND pa.address_id = (
    SELECT MAX(pa2.address_id) 
    FROM patient_addresses pa2 
    WHERE pa2.patient_id = 1
)

SELECT learner_code, gender, resource_key, FeedbackType, UserFeedback
FROM (
    SELECT l.learner_code, l.gender, r.resource_key, 'Regular Feedback' AS FeedbackType, r.feedback AS UserFeedback 
    FROM learners AS l 
    INNER JOIN resource_usage AS r ON l.learner_code = r.learner_code 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT l.learner_code, l.gender, 0 AS resource_key, 'Synthetic Feedback' AS FeedbackType, 
           CASE WHEN o.learner_satisfaction_score < 3 THEN 'Negative' ELSE 'Positive' END AS UserFeedback 
    FROM learners AS l 
    INNER JOIN outcomes AS o ON l.learner_code = o.learner_code
) AS combined_results
WHERE UserFeedback = 'Negative'

SELECT * FROM city 
ORDER BY 
    CASE 
        WHEN name GLOB '[A-Za-z]*' THEN UPPER(name) 
        WHEN name IS NULL THEN '' 
        ELSE UPPER(name) 
    END,
    CASE 
        WHEN INSTR(name, '-') > 0 AND LENGTH(SUBSTR(name, INSTR(name, '-') + 1)) > 0 
        THEN CAST(SUBSTR(name, INSTR(name, '-') + 1) AS INTEGER) 
        ELSE NULL 
    END,
    CASE 
        WHEN INSTR(name, '(') > 0 AND INSTR(name, ')') > INSTR(name, '(')
        THEN CAST(SUBSTR(name, INSTR(name, '(') + 1, INSTR(name, ')') - INSTR(name, '(') - 1) AS INTEGER) 
        ELSE NULL 
    END

SELECT c.name, c.code, GROUP_CONCAT(cn.othername) AS alternative_names 
FROM country c
LEFT JOIN countryothername cn ON c.code = cn.country
GROUP BY c.name, c.code
ORDER BY c.name ASC

SELECT ol.institution_name 
FROM institution_location AS ol, learner_institution_location AS l 
WHERE (ol.x - l.x) * (ol.x - l.x) + (ol.y - l.y) * (ol.y - l.y) <= 1

SELECT 
    p.tissue_source_site,
    COUNT(*) AS total_patients,
    SUM(CASE WHEN p.informed_consent_verified = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS consent_verified,
    ROUND(
        CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN p.informed_consent_verified = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) * 100 / 
        CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT), 2
    ) AS consent_verified_percentage
FROM patients p
GROUP BY p.tissue_source_site
ORDER BY p.tissue_source_site

WITH sales AS (
    SELECT "gasstationid" AS "GasstationId", 
           "customerid" AS "CustomerId", 
           "date" AS "SaleDate", 
           "amount" AS "Amount" 
    FROM "transactions_1k"
), 
initial_purchase AS (
    SELECT "GasstationId", 
           "CustomerId", 
           MIN("SaleDate") AS "FirstPurchaseDate" 
    FROM sales 
    GROUP BY "GasstationId", "CustomerId"
), 
customer_wages AS (
    SELECT s.* 
    FROM sales AS s 
    JOIN initial_purchase AS i 
        ON s."CustomerId" = i."CustomerId" 
        AND s."GasstationId" = i."GasstationId"

WITH RECURSIVE set_hierarchy AS (
    -- Anchor: Start with sets that have a parent
    SELECT 
        id,
        code,
        parentCode,
        CAST(code AS TEXT) AS ancestral_path,
        1 AS level
    FROM sets 
    WHERE parentCode IS NOT NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive: Join with parent sets to build the path upward
    SELECT 
        s.id,
        s.code,
        s.parentCode,
        CAST(sh.ancestral_path || '-' || s.parentCode AS TEXT) AS ancestral_path,
        sh.level + 1
    FROM sets s
    INNER JOIN set_hierarchy sh ON s.parentCode = sh.code
)
SELECT 
    id,
    code,
    ancestral_path
FROM set_hierarchy
WHERE level = (
    SELECT MAX(level) 
    FROM set_hierarchy sh2 
    WHERE sh2.id = set_hierarchy.id
)
ORDER BY id

SELECT 
    link_to_event,
    CASE 
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN 'Closed'
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 
             AND SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN 'Open'
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN 'Open'
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN 'Planning'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
    END AS consolidated_status,
    CASE 
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN 
            MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Closed' THEN remaining ELSE NULL END)
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 
             AND SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN 
            MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN spent ELSE 0 END) + 
            MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN spent ELSE 0 END)
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN 
            MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Open' THEN spent ELSE NULL END)
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN 
            MAX(CASE WHEN event_status = 'Planning' THEN spent ELSE NULL END)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS financial_value
FROM budget
GROUP BY link_to_event

WITH monthly_purchases AS (

    FROM purchase
),
ordered_purchases AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id, 
        purchase_date,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY purchase_date) as rn
    FROM monthly_purchases
),
streak_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        purchase_date,
        rn,
        -- Create a group identifier for consecutive months
        strftime('%Y%m', purchase_date) - rn as group_id
    FROM ordered_purchases
),
user_streaks AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        COUNT(*) as streak_length
    FROM streak_groups
    GROUP BY user_id, group_id
),
longest_streaks AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        MAX(streak_length) as longest_streak
    FROM user_streaks
    GROUP BY user_id
)
SELECT 
    longest_streak as streak_length,
    COUNT(*) as user_count
FROM longest_streaks
GROUP BY longest_streak
ORDER BY streak_length DESC

WITH RECURSIVE BottomUpCalculation AS (
    -- Start with all nodes, including their basic info
    SELECT 
        entityid,
        parentid,
        emission,
        pctcontribution,
        emission AS cumulative_emission,
        0 AS level
    FROM tree_table
    WHERE entityid NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT parentid FROM tree_table WHERE parentid IS NOT NULL)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Build up the hierarchy level by level
    SELECT 
        t.entityid,
        t.parentid,
        t.emission,
        t.pctcontribution,
        CAST(t.emission + COALESCE(
            (SELECT SUM(c.cumulative_emission * c.pctcontribution / 100.0)
             FROM BottomUpCalculation c
             JOIN tree_table orig ON c.entityid = orig.entityid
             WHERE orig.parentid = t.entityid), 0) AS REAL) AS cumulative_emission,
        b.level + 1
    FROM tree_table t
    JOIN BottomUpCalculation b ON t.entityid = b.parentid
)
SELECT entityid, parentid, cumulative_emission AS total_emission
FROM (
    SELECT entityid, parentid, cumulative_emission,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY entityid ORDER BY level DESC) as rn
    FROM BottomUpCalculation
) ranked
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY entityid

WITH RECURSIVE date_series AS (
    -- Generate all dates from 2010-01-01 to 2014-12-31
    SELECT date('2010-01-01') as date_val
    UNION ALL
    SELECT date(date_val, '+1 day')
    FROM date_series
    WHERE date_val < '2014-12-31'
),
user_nonworking_days AS (
    SELECT 
        u.id,
        u.displayname,
        u.age,
        COUNT(*) as non_working_days
    FROM users u
    JOIN date_series ds ON ds.date_val >= date(u.creationdate) 
                        AND ds.date_val <= date(u.lastaccessdate)
                        AND ds.date_val >= '2010-01-01' 
                        AND ds.date_val <= '2014-12-31'
    WHERE u.creationdate >= '2010-01-01' 
      AND u.lastaccessdate <= '2014-12-31'
      AND (
          -- Weekend days (Saturday = 6, Sunday = 0 in SQLite strftime)
          strftime('%w', ds.date_val) IN ('0', '6')
          OR
          -- Specific holidays
          (
              (strftime('%m-%d', ds.date_val) = '01-01' AND strftime('%Y', ds.date_val) BETWEEN '2010' AND '2014') -- New Year's Day
              OR
              (strftime('%m-%d', ds.date_val) = '07-04' AND strftime('%Y', ds.date_val) BETWEEN '2010' AND '2014') -- Independence Day
              OR
              (strftime('%m-%d', ds.date_val) = '11-11' AND strftime('%Y', ds.date_val) BETWEEN '2010' AND '2014') -- Veterans Day
              OR
              (strftime('%m-%d', ds.date_val) = '12-25' AND strftime('%Y', ds.date_val) BETWEEN '2010' AND '2014') -- Christmas Day
          )
      )
    GROUP BY u.id, u.displayname, u.age
)
SELECT 
    id,
    displayname,
    age,
    non_working_days
FROM user_nonworking_days
ORDER BY id

WITH RECURSIVE atom_hierarchy AS (
    -- Base case: start with the initial atom
    SELECT 
        c.atom_id, 
        c.atom_id2, 
        c.bond_id, 
        1 AS level, 
        ',' || c.atom_id || ',' || c.atom_id2 || ',' AS visited
    FROM connected c 
    WHERE c.atom_id = 'TR007_8'
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: find connections from the current atom_id2
    SELECT 
        c.atom_id, 
        c.atom_id2, 
        c.bond_id, 
        ah.level + 1, 
        ah.visited || c.atom_id2 || ','
    FROM connected c 
    JOIN atom_hierarchy ah ON c.atom_id = ah.atom_id2 
    WHERE ah.level < 10  -- Prevent infinite recursion with reasonable limit
      AND INSTR(ah.visited, ',' || c.atom_id2 || ',') = 0  -- Cycle detection
)
SELECT DISTINCT atom_id, atom_id2, bond_id, level 
FROM atom_hierarchy 
ORDER BY level, atom_id

WITH atom_data AS (
    SELECT 
        atom_id AS id,
        molecule_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN element = 'cl' THEN UPPER(element) 
            ELSE element 
        END AS processed_value
    FROM atom
),
bond_data AS (
    SELECT 
        bond_id AS id,
        molecule_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN bond_type = 'single' THEN '-'
            WHEN bond_type = 'double' THEN '='
            WHEN bond_type = 'triple' THEN '#'
            ELSE bond_type 
        END AS processed_value
    FROM bond
),
combined_data AS (
    -- Get all atoms with their element values
    SELECT 
        id,
        molecule_id,
        processed_value AS val
    FROM atom_data
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Get all bonds with their bond_type values
    SELECT 
        id,
        molecule_id,
        processed_value AS val
    FROM bond_data
)
SELECT 
    cd.id,
    cd.molecule_id,
    cd.val
FROM combined_data cd
ORDER BY 
    cd.molecule_id,
    CASE WHEN cd.id IN (SELECT atom_id FROM atom) THEN 1 ELSE 2 END,
    cd.id

    FROM event

type_year_ranges AS (
    SELECT 
        event_type,
        MIN(event_year) AS earliest_year,
        MAX(event_year) AS latest_year
    FROM event_years
    GROUP BY event_type
),
type_year_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        e.type AS event_type,
        CAST(strftime('%Y', e.event_date) AS INTEGER) AS event_year,
        COUNT(*) AS event_count
    FROM event e
    WHERE e.event_date IS NOT NULL AND e.type IS NOT NULL

)
SELECT 
    tyr.event_type,
    COALESCE(tyc_latest.event_count, 0) - COALESCE(tyc_earliest.event_count, 0) AS year_difference
FROM type_year_ranges tyr
LEFT JOIN type_year_counts tyc_earliest 
    ON tyr.event_type = tyc_earliest.event_type 
    AND tyr.earliest_year = tyc_earliest.event_year
LEFT JOIN type_year_counts tyc_latest

WITH RECURSIVE split_dates(id, original_date, remaining, processed_dates, pos) AS (
  -- Base case: select records that need splitting
  SELECT 
    id,
    date as original_date,
    date as remaining,
    '' as processed_dates,
    1 as pos
  FROM Player_Attributes 
  WHERE date LIKE '% %'
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursive case: extract next date
  SELECT 
    id,
    original_date,
    CASE 
      WHEN INSTR(remaining, ' ') > 0 THEN
        CASE 
          WHEN INSTR(SUBSTR(remaining, INSTR(remaining, ' ') + 1), ' ') > 0 THEN
            SUBSTR(remaining, INSTR(SUBSTR(remaining, INSTR(remaining, ' ') + 1), ' ') + INSTR(remaining, ' ') + 1)
          ELSE ''
        END
      ELSE ''
    END as remaining,
    CASE 
      WHEN INSTR(remaining, ' ') > 0 THEN
        CASE 
          WHEN INSTR(SUBSTR(remaining, INSTR(remaining, ' ') + 1), ' ') > 0 THEN
            TRIM(SUBSTR(remaining, 1, INSTR(SUBSTR(remaining, INSTR(remaining, ' ') + 1), ' ') + INSTR(remaining, ' ') - 1))
          ELSE TRIM(remaining)
        END
      ELSE TRIM(remaining)
    END as processed_dates,
    pos + 1
  FROM split_dates
  WHERE remaining != '' AND pos < 10  -- Prevent infinite recursion
)

SELECT id, processed_dates as date_part
FROM split_dates
WHERE processed_dates != ''
UNION ALL
-- Include records that don't need splitting
SELECT id, date as date_part
FROM Player_Attributes
WHERE date NOT LIKE '% %'
ORDER BY id, date_part

-- Get unique activation months and their numeric representations
activation_months AS (
  SELECT 
    STRFTIME('MON-%y', act_date) AS activation_month,
    STRFTIME('%Y%m', act_date) AS month_key
  FROM tbl
  GROUP BY STRFTIME('MON-%y', act_date), STRFTIME('%Y%m', act_date)
),

-- Create a grid of all activation months vs all subsequent months
month_grid AS (
  SELECT 
    a1.activation_month AS activation_month,
    a1.month_key AS activation_key,
    a2.activation_month AS actual_month,
    a2.month_key AS actual_key
  FROM activation_months a1
  CROSS JOIN activation_months a2
  WHERE a1.month_key <= a2.month_key
),

-- Link each molecule to its activation month (based on when it was first created/activated)
molecule_activations AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT
    m.molecule_id,
    STRFTIME('%Y%m', t.act_date) AS activation_month_key,
    STRFTIME('MON-%y', t.act_date) AS activation_month_name
  FROM tbl t
  CROSS JOIN molecule m
  -- This creates a mapping of all molecules to all activation dates for demonstration
  -- In a real scenario, you'd have a proper relationship between molecules and their creation dates
),

-- For each activation month, get all molecules that should be active in each subsequent month
active_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    mg.activation_month,
    mg.actual_month,
    COUNT(DISTINCT ma.molecule_id) AS active_molecules
  FROM month_grid mg
  LEFT JOIN molecule_activations ma ON (
    ma.activation_month_key = mg.activation_key
    AND ma.activation_month_key <= mg.actual_key
  )
  GROUP BY mg.activation_month, mg.actual_month, mg.activation_key, mg.actual_key
)

SELECT 
  activation_month,
  actual_month,
  active_molecules

SELECT valid_from, valid_until, count
FROM (
    SELECT 
        dt AS valid_from,
        DATE(LEAD(dt) OVER (ORDER BY dt), '-1 day') AS valid_until,
        SUM(count_change) OVER (ORDER BY dt) AS count
    FROM (
        -- Start events (add 1 to count)
        SELECT valid_from AS dt, 1 AS count_change
        FROM (
            SELECT DATE('2014-01-01') AS valid_from, DATE('2014-01-31') AS valid_until 
            UNION ALL 
            SELECT DATE('2014-02-01') AS valid_from, DATE('2014-02-28') AS valid_until
        )
        UNION ALL
        -- End events (subtract 1 from count)
        SELECT DATE(valid_until, '+1 day') AS dt, -1 AS count_change
        FROM (
            SELECT DATE('2014-01-01') AS valid_from, DATE('2014-01-31') AS valid_until 
            UNION ALL 
            SELECT DATE('2014-02-01') AS valid_from, DATE('2014-02-28') AS valid_until
        )
    )
)
WHERE count > 0 
  AND valid_until IS NOT NULL 
  AND valid_from <= valid_until

WITH RECURSIVE 
string1_split(value, rest) AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR('24,36,78,98', ',') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR('24,36,78,98', 1, INSTR('24,36,78,98', ',') - 1)
            ELSE '24,36,78,98'
        END,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR('24,36,78,98', ',') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR('24,36,78,98', INSTR('24,36,78,98', ',') + 1) || ','
            ELSE ''
        END
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(rest, ',') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR(rest, 1, INSTR(rest, ',') - 1)
            ELSE rest
        END,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(rest, ',') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR(rest, INSTR(rest, ',') + 1)
            ELSE ''
        END
    FROM string1_split 
    WHERE LENGTH(rest) > 0
),
string2_split(value, rest) AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR('25,36,88,98', ',') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR('25,36,88,98', 1, INSTR('25,36,88,98', ',') - 1)
            ELSE '25,36,88,98'
        END,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR('25,36,88,98', ',') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR('25,36,88,98', INSTR('25,36,88,98', ',') + 1) || ','
            ELSE ''
        END
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(rest, ',') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR(rest, 1, INSTR(rest, ',') - 1)
            ELSE rest
        END,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(rest, ',') > 0 
            THEN SUBSTR(rest, INSTR(rest, ',') + 1)
            ELSE ''
        END
    FROM string2_split 
    WHERE LENGTH(rest) > 0
)
SELECT DISTINCT s1.value
FROM string1_split s1
WHERE LENGTH(s1.value) > 0
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM string2_split s2 
    WHERE LENGTH(s2.value) > 0 
      AND s1.value = s2.value
  )

            PARTITION BY substr("date", 1, 10), "match_api_id",

WITH match_times AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        date,
        CAST(SUBSTR(date, 12, 2) AS REAL) + CAST(SUBSTR(date, 15, 2) AS REAL) / 60.0 AS hour_of_day,
        DATE(date) AS match_date_str 
    FROM match
    WHERE date IS NOT NULL
),
target_times AS (
    SELECT 8 AS target_hour, 'morning' AS time_period
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 20 AS target_hour, 'evening' AS time_period
),
distance_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        mt.id,
        mt.date,
        mt.match_date_str,
        tt.target_hour,
        tt.time_period,
        ABS(mt.hour_of_day - tt.target_hour) AS time_distance,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY mt.match_date_str, tt.target_hour 
            ORDER BY ABS(mt.hour_of_day - tt.target_hour), mt.id
        ) AS time_rank
    FROM match_times AS mt
    CROSS JOIN target_times AS tt
)
SELECT 
    dc.match_date_str,
    dc.id AS match_id,
    dc.date,
    dc.time_period,
    dc.time_distance
FROM distance_calc AS dc
WHERE dc.time_rank = 1
ORDER BY dc.match_date_str, dc.time_period

WITH RECURSIVE my_table AS (
    SELECT 'A' AS cto, 1 AS nif, 'A1' AS file_, 5 AS filesize 
    UNION ALL SELECT 'A' AS cto, 1 AS nif, 'A2' AS file_, 3 AS filesize 
    UNION ALL SELECT 'A' AS cto, 2 AS nif, 'A3' AS file_, 2 AS filesize 
    UNION ALL SELECT 'A' AS cto, 2 AS nif, 'A4' AS file_, 1 AS filesize 
    UNION ALL SELECT 'B' AS cto, 1 AS nif, 'B1' AS file_, 4 AS filesize 
    UNION ALL SELECT 'B' AS cto, 1 AS nif, 'B2' AS file_, 6 AS filesize
),
ordered_data AS (
    SELECT 
        cto, 
        nif, 
        file_, 
        filesize,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY cto ORDER BY file_) as rn
    FROM my_table
),
grouped_identifiers AS (
    SELECT 
        cto,
        nif,
        MIN(rn) as first_rn,
        MAX(rn) as last_rn,
        SUM(filesize) as group_size,
        COUNT(*) as group_count
    FROM ordered_data
    GROUP BY cto, nif
),
partition_builder AS (
    -- Base case: start with the first identifier group
    SELECT 
        cto,
        first_rn,
        last_rn,
        group_size,
        group_count,
        1 as partition_num,
        group_size as partition_size,
        first_rn as next_expected_rn
    FROM grouped_identifiers g1
    WHERE first_rn = (
        SELECT MIN(first_rn) 
        FROM grouped_identifiers g2 
        WHERE g2.cto = g1.cto
    )
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: add subsequent identifier groups
    SELECT 
        g.cto,
        g.first_rn,
        g.last_rn,
        g.group_size,
        g.group_count,
        CASE 
            WHEN p.partition_size + g.group_size > 10 OR 
                 p.next_expected_rn != g.first_rn OR
                 (SELECT SUM(group_count) FROM grouped_identifiers 
                  WHERE cto = p.cto AND first_rn >= p.first_rn AND first_rn <= g.first_rn) > 7
            THEN p.partition_num + 1
            ELSE p.partition_num
        END as partition_num,
        CASE 
            WHEN p.partition_size + g.group_size > 10 OR 
                 p.next_expected_rn != g.first_rn OR
                 (SELECT SUM(group_count) FROM grouped_identifiers 
                  WHERE cto = p.cto AND first_rn >= p.first_rn AND first_rn <= g.first_rn) > 7
            THEN g.group_size
            ELSE p.partition_size + g.group_size
        END as partition_size,
        g.last_rn + 1 as next_expected_rn
    FROM grouped_identifiers g
    JOIN partition_builder p ON g.cto = p.cto
    WHERE g.first_rn = (
        SELECT MIN(first_rn) 
        FROM grouped_identifiers g2 
        WHERE g2.cto = p.cto AND g2.first_rn > p.last_rn
    )
),
final_partitions AS (
    SELECT 
        o.cto,
        o.nif,
        o.file_,
        o.filesize,
        o.rn,
        p.partition_num,
        SUM(o.filesize) OVER (PARTITION BY o.cto, p.partition_num) as total_partition_size
    FROM ordered_data o
    JOIN partition_builder p ON o.cto = p.cto AND o.rn BETWEEN p.first_rn AND p.last_rn
),
subpartitioned AS (
    SELECT 
        cto,
        nif,
        file_,
        filesize,
        partition_num,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY cto, partition_num ORDER BY rn) as subpartition_num,
        total_partition_size
    FROM final_partitions
)
SELECT 
    cto as category,
    nif as identifier,
    file_ as file_name,
    partition_num,
    subpartition_num,
    total_partition_size
FROM subpartitioned
ORDER BY cto, partition_num, subpartition_num

WITH RECURSIVE "a" AS (
    SELECT 'Jack|Sparrow|17-09-16|DY7009|Address at some where|details' AS "test" 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 'Jack|Sparrow|17-09-16|DY7009|' 
    UNION ALL 
    SELECT 'Jack|Sparrow|17-09-16'
),
splitter AS (
    SELECT 
        "test",
        "test" AS remaining,
        '' AS result,
        0 AS pipe_count
    FROM "a"
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        "test",
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(remaining, '|') > 0 THEN SUBSTR(remaining, INSTR(remaining, '|') + 1)
            ELSE ''
        END AS remaining,
        CASE 
            WHEN INSTR(remaining, '|') > 0 THEN 
                result || SUBSTR(remaining, 1, INSTR(remaining, '|'))
            ELSE result || remaining
        END AS result,
        pipe_count + 1
    FROM splitter
    WHERE pipe_count < 5 AND remaining != ''
)
SELECT 
    "test",
    MAX(result) AS "res"
FROM splitter
WHERE pipe_count = 5 OR remaining = ''
GROUP BY "test"

WITH player_dates AS (
    SELECT 
        player_fifa_api_id AS id, 
        MIN(DATE(date)) AS first_date, 
        MAX(DATE(date)) AS last_date 
    FROM Player_Attributes 
    WHERE player_fifa_api_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY player_fifa_api_id
),
date_bounds AS (
    SELECT 
        DATE('2000-01-01') AS start_date,
        DATE('2025-12-31') AS end_date
),
gap_analysis AS (
    SELECT 
        pd.id,
        CASE 
            WHEN pd.first_date > db.start_date THEN 'Before First Date'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS gap_type,
        CASE 
            WHEN pd.first_date > db.start_date THEN pd.first_date
            ELSE NULL
        END AS relevant_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN pd.first_date > db.start_date THEN julianday(pd.first_date) - julianday(db.start_date)
            ELSE NULL
        END AS gap_days
    FROM player_dates pd
    CROSS JOIN date_bounds db
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        pd.id,
        CASE 
            WHEN pd.last_date < db.end_date THEN 'After Last Date'
            ELSE NULL
        END AS gap_type,
        CASE 
            WHEN pd.last_date < db.end_date THEN pd.last_date
            ELSE NULL
        END AS relevant_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN pd.last_date < db.end_date THEN julianday(db.end_date) - julianday(pd.last_date)
            ELSE NULL
        END AS gap_days
    FROM player_dates pd
    CROSS JOIN date_bounds db
)
SELECT 
    id,
    gap_type,
    relevant_date,
    CAST(gap_days AS INTEGER) AS gap_days
FROM gap_analysis
WHERE gap_type IS NOT NULL AND gap_days IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY id, gap_type

        1 AS atom_number, 
        atom_count 
    FROM (
        SELECT

            COUNT(atom_id) AS atom_count 
        FROM atom 
        GROUP BY molecule_id
    ) AS base 
    WHERE atom_count > 0  -- Only include molecules that have atoms
    
    UNION ALL 
    
    SELECT 
        mar.molecule_id, 
        mar.atom_number + 1, 
        mar.atom_count 
    FROM molecule_atoms_recursive AS mar
    WHERE mar.atom_number < mar.atom_count  -- Termination condition
)

    atom_number 
FROM molecule_atoms_recursive

            THEN INSTR(SUBSTR(version, INSTR(version, '.') + INSTR(SUBSTR(version, INSTR(version, '.') + 1), '.') + 1), '-') - 1
            WHEN INSTR(SUBSTR(version, INSTR(version, '.') + INSTR(SUBSTR(version, INSTR(version, '.') + 1), '.') + 1), '.') > 0

INSERT INTO groups (ROOM_GUID, USER_GUID, permissions, membership_status)
SELECT 
    l.guid AS ROOM_GUID,
    g.USER_GUID,
    g.permissions,
    g.membership_status
FROM login l
JOIN (
    -- Find duplicate login/name pairs that have at least one entry in groups table
    SELECT login, name
    FROM login
    WHERE (login, name) IN (
        SELECT login, name 
        FROM login 
        GROUP BY login, name 
        HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
    )
    AND guid IN (SELECT ROOM_GUID FROM groups)
) dup ON l.login = dup.login AND l.name = dup.name
JOIN groups g ON g.ROOM_GUID = (
    -- Get the existing room_guid for this duplicate pair
    SELECT ROOM_GUID 
    FROM groups g2 
    JOIN login l2 ON g2.ROOM_GUID = l2.guid 
    WHERE l2.login = dup.login AND l2.name = dup.name 
    LIMIT 1
)
WHERE l.guid NOT IN (SELECT ROOM_GUID FROM groups)

        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY l.id ORDER BY l.date DESC) AS rn

SELECT

WITH RaceResults AS (
    SELECT 
        r.raceId, 
        r.year, 
        r.name AS raceName, 
        res.positionText, 
        d.forename || ' ' || d.surname AS driverName, 
        res.driverId, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY r.raceId ORDER BY CAST(res.positionText AS INTEGER)) AS position_rank 
    FROM results AS res 
    INNER JOIN races AS r ON res.raceId = r.raceId 
    INNER JOIN drivers AS d ON res.driverId = d.driverId 
    WHERE res.positionText IN ('1', '2', '3')
)
SELECT 
    rr.raceId, 
    MAX(rr.year) AS year, 
    MAX(rr.raceName) AS raceName, 
    MAX(CASE WHEN rr.positionText = '1' THEN rr.driverName END) AS position1_driver, 
    MAX(CASE WHEN rr.positionText = '1' THEN rr.driverId END) AS position1, 
    MAX(CASE WHEN rr.positionText = '2' THEN rr.driverName END) AS position2_driver, 
    MAX(CASE WHEN rr.positionText = '2' THEN rr.driverId END) AS position2, 
    MAX(CASE WHEN rr.positionText = '3' THEN rr.driverName END) AS position3_driver, 
    MAX(CASE WHEN rr.positionText = '3' THEN rr.driverId END) AS position3 
FROM RaceResults AS rr 
WHERE rr.position_rank = CAST(rr.positionText AS INTEGER)
GROUP BY rr.raceId 
ORDER BY MAX(rr.year) DESC, rr.raceId

WITH status_counts AS (

    FROM results r
    JOIN status s ON r.statusId = s.statusId
    GROUP BY r.driverId, r.statusId, s.status
)
SELECT 
    driverId,
    status AS most_common_status,
    status_count AS frequency
FROM status_counts 
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY driverId

WITH DriverGroups AS (SELECT CASE WHEN nationality IS NULL THEN 'Unknown' ELSE nationality END AS driver_group, code FROM drivers WHERE NOT code IS NULL), GroupedCodes AS (SELECT driver_group, MIN(code) AS min_code, MAX(code) AS max_code, COUNT(DISTINCT code) AS distinct_code_count FROM DriverGroups GROUP BY driver_group) SELECT driver_group, CASE WHEN distinct_code_count > 1 THEN min_code || '-' || max_code ELSE max_code END AS concat_codes FROM GroupedCodes GROUP BY driver_group, distinct_code_count, min_code, max_code ORDER BY driver_group

WITH DriverGroups AS (
    SELECT 
        CASE WHEN nationality IS NULL THEN 'Unknown' ELSE nationality END AS driver_group, 
        code 
    FROM drivers 
    WHERE code IS NOT NULL
), GroupedCodes AS (
    SELECT 
        driver_group, 
        MIN(code) AS min_code, 
        MAX(code) AS max_code, 
        COUNT(DISTINCT code) AS distinct_code_count 
    FROM DriverGroups 
    GROUP BY driver_group
) 
SELECT 
    driver_group, 
    CASE 
        WHEN distinct_code_count > 1 THEN min_code || '-' || max_code 
        ELSE min_code 
    END AS concat_codes 
FROM GroupedCodes

WITH TeamAverages AS (
    SELECT 
        AVG(CAST(buildUpPlaySpeed AS REAL)) AS AvgBuildUpPlaySpeed, 
        AVG(CAST(defenceTeamWidth AS REAL)) AS AvgDefenceTeamWidth 
    FROM Team_Attributes 
    WHERE buildUpPlaySpeed IS NOT NULL AND defenceTeamWidth IS NOT NULL
) 
SELECT 
    TA.id, 
    TA.buildUpPlaySpeed, 
    TA.buildUpPlayPassing, 
    TA.chanceCreationPassing, 
    TA.chanceCreationCrossing, 
    TA.chanceCreationShooting, 
    TA.defencePressure, 
    TA.defenceAggression, 
    TA.defenceTeamWidth 
FROM Team_Attributes AS TA
CROSS JOIN TeamAverages
WHERE 
    CAST(TA.buildUpPlaySpeed AS REAL) > TeamAverages.AvgBuildUpPlaySpeed 
    AND CAST(TA.defenceTeamWidth AS REAL) < TeamAverages.AvgDefenceTeamWidth 
ORDER BY TA.buildUpPlaySpeed DESC, TA.defenceTeamWidth ASC

WITH TeamRecords AS (
    SELECT 
        team_api_id,
        id,
        date,
        buildUpPlaySpeed,
        buildUpPlayPassing,
        defenceTeamWidth,
        chanceCreationShooting,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id ORDER BY date) AS rn,
        COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY team_api_id) AS total_records
    FROM Team_Attributes
    WHERE buildUpPlaySpeed IS NOT NULL 
        AND buildUpPlayPassing IS NOT NULL 
        AND defenceTeamWidth IS NOT NULL 
        AND chanceCreationShooting IS NOT NULL
),
TeamComparisons AS (
    SELECT 
        current.team_api_id,
        current.id,
        current.date,
        current.buildUpPlaySpeed,
        current.buildUpPlayPassing,
        current.defenceTeamWidth,
        current.chanceCreationShooting,
        current.total_records,
        -- Next record (for forward comparison)
        LEAD(current.buildUpPlaySpeed) OVER (PARTITION BY current.team_api_id ORDER BY current.date) AS next_buildUpPlaySpeed,
        LEAD(current.buildUpPlayPassing) OVER (PARTITION BY current.team_api_id ORDER BY current.date) AS next_buildUpPlayPassing,
        -- Previous record (for backward comparison)
        LAG(current.defenceTeamWidth) OVER (PARTITION BY current.team_api_id ORDER BY current.date) AS prev_defenceTeamWidth,
        LAG(current.chanceCreationShooting) OVER (PARTITION BY current.team_api_id ORDER BY current.date) AS prev_chanceCreationShooting
    FROM TeamRecords current
)
SELECT 
    id,
    buildUpPlaySpeed,
    buildUpPlayPassing,
    next_buildUpPlaySpeed,
    next_buildUpPlayPassing,
    defenceTeamWidth,
    chanceCreationShooting,
    prev_defenceTeamWidth,
    prev_chanceCreationShooting
FROM TeamComparisons
WHERE total_records > 1
    AND prev_defenceTeamWidth > buildUpPlaySpeed
ORDER BY team_api_id, date

items AS (

WITH patient_diagnoses AS (
    -- Get all relevant diagnosis records from both Patient and Examination tables
    SELECT 
        id,
        "First Date" AS diagnosis_date,
        diagnosis AS current_diagnosis,
        NULL AS prev_diagnosis
    FROM patient
    WHERE diagnosis IN ('RA susp.', 'PSS', 'SLE')
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        e.id,
        e."Examination Date" AS diagnosis_date,
        e.diagnosis AS current_diagnosis,
        NULL AS prev_diagnosis
    FROM examination e
    WHERE e.diagnosis IN ('RA susp.', 'PSS', 'SLE')
),

ordered_diagnoses AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        diagnosis_date,
        current_diagnosis,
        LAG(current_diagnosis) OVER (PARTITION BY id ORDER BY diagnosis_date) AS prev_diagnosis
    FROM patient_diagnoses
),

diagnosis_changes AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        diagnosis_date,
        current_diagnosis,
        prev_diagnosis,
        CASE 
            WHEN prev_diagnosis IS NULL OR prev_diagnosis != current_diagnosis 
            THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS is_new_diagnosis
    FROM ordered_diagnoses
)

SELECT 
    id,
    MIN(diagnosis_date) AS first_diagnosis_change_date,
    current_diagnosis
FROM diagnosis_changes
WHERE is_new_diagnosis = 1
GROUP BY id, current_diagnosis
ORDER BY id, first_diagnosis_change_date

    UNION ALL SELECT 4, '2023-02-02', 25, 1 
    UNION ALL SELECT 5, '2023-01-03', 30, 2 
    UNION ALL SELECT 6, '2023-02-03', 40, 2
),
monthly_totals AS (
    SELECT 
        strftime('%Y-%m', activity_date) as month,
        SUM(activity_value) as month_total
    FROM hero_activity
    GROUP BY strftime('%Y-%m', activity_date)
),
ordered_months AS (
    SELECT 
        month,
        month_total,
        LAG(month_total, 1) OVER (ORDER BY month) as prev_month_total

),
activity_with_month AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        activity_date,
        activity_value,
        strftime('%Y-%m', activity_date) as month,
        SUM(activity_value) OVER (ORDER BY activity_date) AS run_tot,

)

WITH RaceResults AS (
